ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Removal Of Trustees, Accounting And For Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar May-26-2010 Continued To Jun-09-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Latham for and with Robert Bunje and for Barry Bunshoft. Amanda Ebey and Mark Webb for and with Marcia Kimpton and for Drennon Kimpton.

Ms. Ebey stated that Drennon Kimpton was unable to attend and that Drennon signed a declaration with regard to the settlement. Mr. Latham addressed feasabilty of the Trust, spoke of monthly distribution, annual income, principal, the purchase of property and stated that the trust/settlement has a reasonable outcome, that it has a tax advantage and is sustainable, and that the Beneficiaries reviewed the accountings and that they are satisifed. Ms. Ebey stated that Drennon agreed to the fixed terms, that she understood her rights, that there would be a tremendous savings to the trust and that Marcia and Drennon were very close and planned to live together in the property. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Off Calendar Feb-25-2010 Advanced From Apr-28-10 To Mar-10-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Removal Of Trustees, Accounting And For Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar Mar-10-2010 Continued To May-26-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Latham for Barry Bunshoft anf Robert Bunje. Amanda Ebey for Marcia Kimpton.

Attorney Mark Webb was not able to attend today's hearing due to a medical emergency. Ms. Ebey confirmed that the Declaratory Relief Petition was withdrawn and stated that the Petition to Terminate the Trust had not been filed. Mr. Latham stated that the trial would take more than two days, that he needed three months to do discovery and suggested a July/August trial date. The Court informed Counsel that the matter would be referred to the Department of the Presiding Judge for trial purposes. The matter was continued to May 26, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery. If the Petition to Terminate the Trust is filed, there will be oral argument only and it will be heard at 10:30 a.m. An evidentiary hearing will not be required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Mar-10-2010 Continued To May-26-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Off Calendar Feb-25-2010 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Feb-10-2010 Continued To Mar-10-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Removal Of Trustees, Accounting And For Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar Feb-10-2010 Continued To Mar-10-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Webb and Amanda Ebey for and with Marcia Kimpton. Thomas Latham for and with Robert Bunje and for Barry Bunshoft. Also present were Niel Fineman, Dr. Dean Rider, Kat Taylor and the Daughter of Marcia Kimpton.

Ms. Ebey stated that she had the verifications to the petitions. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to trust funds being used for payment of Mark Webb's attorney's fees. The Motion to allow payment of interim attorney's fees from the Trust is denied without prejudice to renewal at the time of the evidentiary hearing. The Motion asking the Court to prohibit the Trustees from using trust funds for payment of attorney's fees is also denied without prejudice until the time of a further evidentiary hearing. The Court suggested Counsel meet and confer on the issues that are going to be tried and as to the number of Witnesses. The matter was continued to March 10, 2010 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition For Determination That Petitioner'S Proposed Petition For Removal Of Trustees, Accounting;And Damages For Negligence/Breach Of Fiduciary Duty, And Attorneys' Fees And Costs Does Not Constitute A Contest Of Trust.
Off Calendar Nov-25-2009 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Tom Lathan, Esq. for Trustees Barry Bunshoft and Rovbert Bunje. Mark Webb, Esq. and Amanda Ebey for Petitioner Marcia Kimpton.
No objections were made in open court, that the proposed petition does not constitute a contest of the Trust, Order submitted and to be signed after review by the court. After hearing arguments by counsel, matter was continued to Jan. 11, 2010 at 10:30 a.m. for full hearing. Briefing on attorney's fee shall be filed by Jan.5, 2010.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Ex Parte Application For Order Shortening Time On Hearing Of Motion For Safe Harbor And Order Thereon
Off Calendar Nov-02-2009 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Combination Of Trusts
Off Calendar Oct-17-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Ellen Kahn apperaed for Barry Bunshoft (petitioner) and Robert Bunje (Petitioner).

The Court expressed concern about the beneficiaries giving up their right for Power of Appointment. Ms. Kahn explained in detail that having four trusts required four seperate accountings, four tax preparations, four different check books and that it made things costly and complicated. She further explained that the Power of Appoinment would be lost on the smaller trust only and that they are requesting the smaller trust go to the larger trust. The Court granted both petitions (signing two orders for the same).

Judge: Herbert Donaldson. Reporter: Patrick Heard CSR#11055. Bailiff: O. Arias. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

Petition For Combination Of Trusts
Off Calendar Oct-17-2007 Granted Per Written Order
